name: When an agent is working on a Goal, the user should have the ability in the web

failed_actions.message: The protected live-tail outcome is not preserved: the initial request sorts ascending with offset 0 and limit 200, returning the oldest 200 entries for large Goals, while the SSE replay filter can suppress the first live Goal log when the stream starts empty.
failed_actions.rule: Architectural evolution is allowed: a candidate satisfies governance when it preserves the protected product outcome or clearly explains why a better design replaces it, with relevant tests and intent documentation updated.

failed_actions.message: Tests check static source strings and server-side SSE serialization but do not exercise activity ordering, pagination, or browser SSE consumption, leaving the live cross-surface behavior unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Use verification proportional to risk: focused tests are sufficient for localized behavior, while cross-surface, persistence, concurrency, release, or recovery changes need corresponding integration evidence and affected documentation updates.

rounds.prompt: When an agent is working on a Goal, the user should have the ability in the web surface to watch the logs in real-time. It should show up in the Toolbar, similar to how the goal Chat works but for logs, like a log tail.
